meta.goal: Build github.com/Autocss-com/autocss = the refined, completed version of `starter`. Measuring stick: the finished app must look, behave, and work like `dhcp` (the working prototype), changing nothing except to improve to the D7460N Architecture. Separate repo so the two originals are never touched.

repos.autocss: this repo - best-of-both: starter's architecture, completed using dhcp as the target, every feature rewritten to D7460N.
repos.starter: the D7460N-correct rewrite of dhcp; CSS-driven visibility done, but incomplete. Architecture reference.
repos.dhcp: messy-but-complete working prototype; the FEATURE + LOOK reference (an MVP, a floor not a ceiling).

rules: CLAUDE.md in this repo is canonical and non-negotiable.
HTML+CSS do everything; JS is CRUD-only (API transport).

JS's single UI bridge is `oninput` on state-machine <input>s - fired by BOTH user action AND programmatically (initial load/restore = select the nav radio: set .checked=true + input.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})) so the input's OWN oninput fires). NEVER click/onclick/change/addEventListener/dynamic import(). dispatchEvent is the SINGLE SANCTIONED exception: used ONLY to make a programmatically-selected nav radio fire its own oninput on load/restore (no real user event exists there); all real user interactions fire oninput natively - no dispatch.
No data-*/class/id; drive dirty/valid/enabled via CSS :has(:valid)/:has(:invalid)/:checked/:empty/:not(:empty)/aria-disabled.
JSON -> elements via toTagName() is allowed; no innerHTML string-building for presentation.
No utils/helpers/utility file names - name each module for what it DOES.
Semantic clarity beats cleverness; modern ES6+ baseline; stateless/idempotent; one API base.
Size: smaller is a guideline not a rule; D7460N compliance may need more code - if autocss ends up larger than dhcp, explain why.
meta.heuristic: Per feature (HTML + companion CSS + companion JS together, companion tracking mandatory): (1) what does it do; (2) is there a MORE MODERN native HTML/CSS way - if yes, STOP and ASK the user before changing markup/CSS, every time (identifying != applying); if no, keep unchanged; (3) will the universal 'one UI to rule them all' app ever need it - YES is the overwhelming default = KEEP & MODERNIZE. Dropping is a rare exception: only if truly redundant, and ALWAYS confirm with user first. Partial/half-built features: inspect, default to COMPLETE not drop.
meta.verify: Enabled env: network=Full (data hosts + Playwright CDN), browser=Playwright chromium via setup script. Tests = JS-logic node unit tests + static/compliance checks + Playwright DOM/behavioral/regression. Pixel-perfect bleeding-edge CSS fidelity needs a HUMAN eye (user does this manually).
meta.cadence: File-by-file, feature-by-feature. Stop and ask on ANY doubt. Check in before FIRST push and after each numbered build step. Test each feature + run regression as we go. Keep companions in lockstep.
meta.principle: USER PRIORITY (restated + elevated 2026-06-15): do the complex things everyone else over-engineers, but in SIMPLE, direct, declarative-first, browser-first, non-complex ways => easy, fast, small, cheap, interoperable. LESS complexity, NEVER more. Every change must REDUCE complexity, not add it; when in doubt the simpler/more-declarative path wins.
future_goals: POST-MVP interoperability demo: build reference instances embedding this ONE UI into each major CMS + framework (WordPress, Drupal, React, Vue, Angular, etc.) to prove it interoperates with the over-engineered/complex systems. ALL instances serve ONE shared instance of this UI from a CDN. The point: declarative-first/browser-first interoperates with everything AND is simpler/faster/smaller/cheaper.
Color-theme selection (next session): persist + restore exactly like nav and color-scheme (state-machine selection -> browser storage).

REBUILD transitions.css as ONE self-contained, drop-in-anywhere transition system (like color-scheme.css). Current one fades elements in at different times + some more than once (observed 2026-06-16); it is not a single independent system. Want: declarative, browser-first, single consistent fade-in on init + on tab/content change, fired once. Currently commented out in index.html (temporary test state).

COLOR-SYSTEM MIGRATION (Phase 1->2): migrate the app's color CONSUMERS (layout.css/a11y.css/typography.css — --bg-active/--txt-*/--bg-panel/--button-bg/row 5% currentcolor) to the Phase-1 CONTRACT names (--fg/--bg/--bg-selected/--bg-hover/--fg-accent/--outline), and convert themes.css into color-theme-<bluehex>.css (OKLCH overrides of those SAME contract names, linked AFTER color-scheme.css). Then the natural CSS cascade does everything: theme present -> themed; theme absent -> Phase-1 system-color fallback. This is the CASCADE model, NOT an @layer/alias 'bridge' (my earlier mischaracterization, corrected 2026-06-16). See meta.color_system.

MODERN-WEB-GUIDANCE PERIODIC REVIEW (advisory): periodically reconcile the GoogleChrome/modern-web-guidance resource (Claude Code plugin) against autocss's current features/capabilities and proactively SUGGEST improvements it surfaces. Advisory only — subordinate to CLAUDE.md + SESSION-HANDOFF; on conflict repo docs win; identify != apply (confirm with user before any change).

color_system.what: An INDEPENDENT, drop-in-anywhere MODULAR COLOR SYSTEM built in phases. Plain CSS cascade; no JS for color values; no special bridge mechanism.
phase_1_color-scheme.css: Standalone file (assets/css/color-scheme.css) that works BY ITSELF with no other CSS/JS.

JOB 1 = rename user-agent/system defaults into intuitive --fg (foreground)/--bg (background) variables (+ link/accent/selected/hover/disabled/muted/outline) that devs can remember. It sets NO literal color VALUES — every token defaults to a CSS SYSTEM COLOR (Canvas/CanvasText/AccentColor/...), so it adapts to OS light/dark + forced-colors for free.
JOB 2 = it is the FALLBACK when no color-theme file is present; the page still looks right on system colors alone.
JOB 3 = it DEFINES the variable NAMES a color-theme file later OVERRIDES via the natural CSS cascade.
Also owns the light/dark MECHANISM: :root{color-scheme: light dark} (Rule 19, system-follow) + the 3-state Light/Dark/System radio override via pure :has().
CONSUMERS must reference these contract names — that is how a theme (or the system-color fallback) reaches the whole app.
phase_2_color-theme-??????.css: Filename color-theme-??????.css where ?????? = the 6-digit HEX of the theme's base color (hex used in the FILENAME for brevity only).
Contents = ONLY CSS variable OVERRIDES of the Phase-1 contract names. Colors written in OKLCH (not hex) so the ENTIRE palette derives from the ONE base color via hue/lightness/alpha.

Override mechanism = NATURAL CSS CASCADE: link the color-theme file AFTER color-scheme.css in <head>; being later it wins. No @layer needed, no cross-file reference — a later file overrides an earlier one just by load order (a CSS superpower).
FIRST theme = the dhcp BLUE. Today's themes.css (the OKLCH palette) is what becomes color-theme-<bluehex>.css.
Swap themes by swapping which color-theme-*.css is linked; remove all theme files -> Phase-1 system-color fallback.
color_system.phase_3_controls: HTML controls / 4x4 hue picker that reads hues from linked color-theme-*.css filenames ([href*="color-theme-"]) + @property + a native color input. Own session.
color_system.migration_reality: APPLIED 2026-06-21 (claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2): consumers (layout/a11y/typography/scrolling/forms.css) migrated to the Phase-1 contract names; themes.css -> color-theme-66ccff.css (variable-overrides-only, @layer color-theme) created + linked AFTER color-scheme.css (themes.css KEPT per user, unlinked). Decisions: --txt-active/--txt-strong->--fg-emphasis (accent-hued, intentional look change); --button-bg/-hover->--bg-hover/--bg-selected; status (--error-*/--success) + form surfaces (--input-bg/--input-border) defined THEME-SIDE ONLY (no system color exists; color-scheme.css no-literals rule preserved); forms.css fully migrated + logged bugs fixed; decoration (body/html ::before) relocated to layout.css; form-label focus to forms.css. PAINTER REVERTED 2026-06-21 (same session, immediately after): removed the round-6 generic painter (label:has(>input:checked/:hover)) from color-scheme.css, keeping the --bg-selected/--bg-hover tokens; moved its ONLY effective paint (the header Light/Dark/System control pill -- every other state-machine control already had an unlayered consumer rule that won over it) into a layout.css header-label consumer rule (hover->--bg-hover, :has(>input:checked)->--bg-selected+--fg-emphasis). color-scheme.css is now contract+foundation only, no component-state painting. PENDING: human pixel-check.

build_order: 1. Repo skeleton + PROGRESS.json + SessionStart hook + CLAUDE.md.
2. CSS base (starter set; reconcile dhcp concerns; inspect candidate-drops diag/stars/hero/_* and confirm with user before dropping).
3. index.html single universal scaffold (incl. dropdown-capable nav + its CSS).
5. Data shaping: schema(dhcp domain for now)/rules/ + concern-named modules (NEVER utils/helpers) - node tests.
7. CRUD UI forms + forms.css(de-data-*) + fallbacks.css + loading.css.
8. Wire dhcp datasets; full behavioral + regression pass in the enabled env.

_self.on_rotate: start progress/log-NNN.ndjson (next number), register it in shards[], update cursor.
_self.never: never edit past records; only append corrections (record type=lesson or note).
_self.resume: read PROGRESS.json first, then the newest shard, to restore context + why.

cursor.last: MODULAR COLOR SYSTEM Part 1, round 6 (2026-06-16, claude/lucid-hawking-E5Ej2): TESTED the color-scheme.css standalone fallback by commenting out themes.css (+transitions.css) in index.html (TEMP test state — both still commented) — body Canvas/CanvasText flip correctly light<->dark. Then EXTENDED color-scheme.css per user: added --bg-selected + --bg-hover (both rgb(from AccentColor r g b / 5%)); --fg-link now = var(--fg-accent) (links=accent); generalized the active indicator into DOM-agnostic selected+hover for state-machine labels (label:has(>input:is([checkbox],[radio]):checked|:hover)); reduced-transparency collapses the tints to Highlight/ButtonFace. BROWSER-VERIFIED: the SELECTED color-scheme control shows a 5% accent pill + accent text in light+dark. KNOWN: nav/rows selected don't show it standalone (layout.css unlayered var(--bg-active) override) -> needs the CONSUMER BRIDGE (now in future_goals). transitions.css rebuild added to backlog. Round 5 (prior): SIMPLIFY per user (Least Power, no session tracking). Deleted hydrateShell() + the isShellHydrated flag + 'Shell hydrated' log; inlined banner/nav-label/version injection as plain sequential calls in the SINGLE on-load function initializeOnInputLifecycle() (runs once from app.js; per-page data still loads via the nav radio's own oninput). Kept nav-label/banner/version text injection exactly as before. Renamed scheme->color-scheme everywhere (index.html name=color-scheme x3, color-scheme.css selectors+comments, oninput.js bindColorSchemeOnInput + selectors); storage key stays camelCase colorScheme. BROWSER-VERIFIED: API-down -> color-scheme persists/restores/round-trips; API-up -> nav labels inject + page data loads (10 rows, H1 Manage) + color-scheme 5/5. FUTURE NOTE: nav items will later be BUILT from JSON (like table cells) instead of a static skeleton. Round 4 (prior): BUGFIX (user-reported: choice not saving cross-session). ROOT CAUSE (reproduced + fixed in a REAL browser via Playwright/chromium): bindSchemeOnInput()+restoreColorScheme() ran AFTER `await hydrateShell()`, so a failed/slow data hydrate skipped them -> radios never bound to persistColorScheme, restore never ran; CSS color flip still worked (pure :has(:checked)) so it LOOKED fine but never persisted. FIX: moved both to run FIRST, BEFORE the await (color is pure UI, must not depend on the data API; scheme radios are static markup at parse time). BROWSER-VERIFIED both API-down (Light now persists+restores despite hydrate throwing) and API-up (5/5: Light saves+restores, System round-trips to OS-follow, no permanent override). Nav binding still gated behind hydrateShell (unchanged; nav needs data). Round 3 (prior): wired JS selection persistence + on-load restore into the EXISTING oninput.js init/storage (no new system). Added (mirroring the nav pair): bindSchemeOnInput() [persists each input[name=scheme].oninput value; NO lifecycle call, NO dispatchEvent], restoreColorScheme() [light/dark -> radio.checked=true, NO dispatch; absent/system -> nothing], getInitialColorScheme()/persistColorScheme() [read/write a colorScheme key BESIDE navigation in the autocss.app.v1 state via read/writePersistent]. Wired into initializeOnInputLifecycle() after the nav bind/trigger. JS sets only .checked, never styles; CSS reacts to :checked. node --check (ESM) passes. DEFERRED still: --txt/--bg/--accent->--fg/--bg consumer bridge; focus-indicator paint; BROWSER VERIFICATION. NOT yet browser-verified.

open_q: PORT-AS-IS is the active strategy (comply-later = THIS BUILD ONLY). Compliance fixes + PWA are SEPARATE future sessions - see SESSION-HANDOFF COMPLIANCE-DEBT LEDGER + FUTURE SESSIONS.

SAVE/DELETE DATA-MODEL DEFECT (HIGH-VALUE; own future session; recommended FIRST): Save succeeds (200) but writes to the WRONG LEVEL - POSTs a new top-level mockapi resource instead of editing record[0].items; Delete never hits the server. 3 causes: (1) read is nested (record[0].items) but write is flat (POST to collection root) - no path writes into record[0].items; (2) id-lookup bug querySelector('label > id') never matches the real <id-> cell (FACT #2) so Save always POSTs + Delete is DOM-only; (3) denormalize puts item field names at resource level. Affects ALL 12 endpoints. FIX = OPTION A (fix id selectors to 'label > id-', carry record[0]'s top-level id via a read accessor in oninput.js, splice item into record[0].items then PUT the whole resource) OR OPTION B (per-item backend routes). Full analysis: NDJSON 2026-06-10 lesson + SESSION-HANDOFF LEDGER. Old Q2 RESOLVED -> FIX (not keep-faithful).
